items.briefing:
Australian staff should be on alert for a spike in tax-themed phishing scams, with Proofpoint warning these attacks are surging locally. Cybercriminals are sending fake emails pretending to be from the ATO or related services, trying to steal personal and financial information. With tax time approaching, these scams are likely to increase, and anyone receiving unexpected emails about tax refunds, debts, or account issues should be especially cautious.

If you receive an email related to your tax affairs, do not click any links — go directly to the ATO website by typing the address into your browser instead.

featured.title: Tax phishing scams surge in Australia, Proofpoint warns
featured.quote: Tax phishing scams targeting Australian financial data surge during end-of-financial-year deadline period.
